title: Memory & Forgetting

analysis.object: T244: Memory and forgetting depth analysis

memory_strength.description: Memory strength scales as log(number of retrievals)

sleep_replay.description: Hippocampal replay at 10x speed during SWS

false_memories.description: DRM paradigm: false recognition of related words
false_memories.reason: Cannot be distinguished from real memories by finite test — EML-∞

reconsolidation.description: Memory becomes labile during retrieval, then restabilizes
reconsolidation.reason: Oscillation between stable and labile states = EML-3 cycle

analysis.act_of_remembering: Is the act of remembering a measurement (EML-2) or oscillation (EML-3)? Answer: BOTH, depending on memory type. Semantic retrieval (facts): EML-2 (measurement — looking up a stored value). Episodic retrieval (experiences): EML-3 (oscillatory reconstruction — replay). The act of forgetting: EML-1 (exponential decay). The threshold from forgetting to not: EML-2 (log-linear recall probability). False memories: EML-∞ (indistinguishable from real by finite test).

analysis.verdict: Forgetting: EML-1. Semantic: EML-2. Episodic replay: EML-3. False memory: EML-∞.
analysis.theorem: T244: Memory Depth — semantic EML-2, episodic EML-3, false memory EML-∞

key_theorem: T244: Memory Depth (S523). Ebbinghaus forgetting: EML-1 (exp decay). Spaced repetition: EML-1 (geometric). Semantic memory: EML-2 (log-organized categories). Episodic replay: EML-3 (oscillatory reconstruction). False memories: EML-∞ (indistinguishable by finite test).
rabbit_hole_log: T244: False memory = EML-∞ (finite test cannot distinguish)
